data.description:
## GHA reconcile-bridge: route inbound writes through the signing seam + inject key

CRITICAL: the reconciler's inbound writer (`_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py`
`_write_event_file`, ~214-259) calls `rebar._store.event_append` DIRECTLY, bypassing
`_seam.append_event`, and stamps only `author` (no `author_email`/`author_id`/`author_sig`).
So Jira-sourced events would classify `unknown-author`/`unsigned` under `verify-identity`.
Config alone is insufficient — this needs a code fix.

- Route inbound event writes through the signing seam (or stamp attribution + sign inline) so reconciler-authored events carry `author_id`/`author_email`/`author_sig` for the Rebar Bot identity. Note: added author fields are additive to the event dict — older readers ignore unknown keys (no format break).
- In `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml`: inject `secrets.REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` to a job-local file, set `identity.signing_key` to it, and set `BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL=joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` (currently `rebar-bridge@users.noreply.github.com`) so git-email resolution maps to the bot identity.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Reconciler inbound writes go through the signing seam (or equivalently stamp `author_id`/`author_email`/`author_sig`); a synced event verifies as `verified` under `rebar verify-identity`.
- [ ] The added author fields are confirmed additive (an older reader ignores them; no store-format break).
- [ ] `reconcile-bridge.yml` injects the signing key securely and sets the committer email to the bot; the key is never logged/committed.
data.title: GHA reconcile-bridge: route inbound writes through signing seam + inject key
